edge: Video moment retrieval and highlight detection (MR/HD) are being spotlighted as the demand for video understanding is drastically increased.
The key objective of MR/HD is to localize the moment and estimate clip-wise accordance level, i.e., saliency score, to the given text query.
The relevance between text query and video contents is sometimes neglected when predicting the moment and its saliency.
To tackle this issue, we introduce Query-Dependent DETR (QD-DETR), a detection transformer tailored for MR/HD.
Our encoding module starts with cross-attention layers to explicitly inject the context of text query into video representation.
To enhance the model's capability of exploiting the query information, we manipulate the video-query pairs to produce irrelevant pairs.
Such negative (irrelevant) video-query pairs are trained to yield low saliency scores, which in turn, encourages the model to estimate precise accordance between query-video pairs.
Lastly, we present an input-adaptive saliency predictor which adaptively defines the criterion of saliency scores for the given video-query pairs.
Our extensive studies verify the importance of building the query-dependent representation for MR/HD.
QD-DETR outperforms state-of-the-art methods on QVHighlights, TVSum, and Charades-STA datasets, verifying the importance of building the query-dependent representation for MR/HD.

Video and query representations extracted from fixed backbones are transformed to be query-dependent using cross-attention layers, suggesting a direct relationship between the two representations for enhancing query-awareness in video processing.

Incorporating irrelevant video-query pairs with low saliency helps enhance the learning objective in processing data by including less relevant pairs for a more holistic understanding of the information.

The saliency token in the transformer encoder-decoder architecture can turn into an adaptive saliency predictor when attended by a specific video instance, showcasing the dynamic nature of processing data and predicting relevant information.

The query representations involve key and value aspects in the cross-attention layers, emphasizing the importance of these elements in generating a comprehensive understanding of queries for data processing.

The encoder function aims to produce clip-wise representations with information on query-relevance, underscoring the significance of these representations in retrieving query-matched moments and predicting outcomes in data processing.
